publication_scope.notes: Issue PDFs are under /sites/default/files and must be accepted only when reached from scoped CLR issue pages.

evidence.notes: No rule disallows CLR issue pages or /sites/default/files PDF assets.
Drupal page sidebar lists issue routes from Volume 17.1 (2010) through Volume 32.2 (2026). Some labels and slugs disagree; neighboring journal navigation is interleaved.
h1 supplies volume/issue/season/year; main content paragraphs contain title text followed by author-linked article PDFs, plus a whole-issue PDF.
Single smoke GET returned a 361,004-byte, 58-page article PDF beginning %PDF-1.7.

reconnaissance.notes: Newest issue discovery and direct PDF delivery pass. The homepage has known navigation defects and historical issue layouts are unsampled.

include: Clinical Law Review issues and article PDFs
scope.notes: Strict path and issue-heading checks are needed because Drupal sidebar navigation interleaves other journals.

entry_points.notes: Homepage doubles as issue archive via sidebar links.

edges.notes: Accept only CLR-prefixed paths; validate displayed label against issue h1 because several labels/slugs disagree.
Article title is paragraph text before br; anchor text is author.

edges.locator: main p:first-of-type a[href$='.pdf'], article p:first-of-type a[href$='.pdf']

archive_patterns.notes: Historical slug punctuation and capitalization vary; follow links rather than synthesize.
Upload month is not the publication date; derive issue date from h1.

pagination.termination: Sidebar list currently ends at Volume 17.1 (Fall 2010).

layout_eras.label: NYU Drupal issue pages
layout_eras.notes: Only newest page sampled; historical markup may vary.

layout_eras.label: Earlier Clinical Law Review backfile
layout_eras.notes: Not exposed by current issue navigation.

sources.locator: article/main p text before br and PDF author anchor
sources.example: LISTENING MIDSTREAM: MID-SEMESTER FEEDBACK FROM STUDENTS FOR CLINICIANS
sources.notes: No article HTML page; title is a text node.

normalization: Preserve source capitalization or title-case in a derived display field

sources.locator: article p a[href$='.pdf'] em, main p a[href$='.pdf'] em
sources.example: Tamar Alexajian, Christine Speidel, & Caroline Wick
sources.notes: Anchor itself is the article PDF.

normalization: Split conservatively on comma and ampersand

abstract.missingness: No abstract or article landing page in sampled route.

sources.notes: Use displayed h1, not upload path.

pages.missingness: Not exposed in HTML; may be parsed from PDF.

sources.example: Tamar Alexajian et al., Listening Midstream, 32 Clinical L. Rev. (Spring 2026)

patterns.locator: article p:first-of-type a[href$='.pdf'], main p:first-of-type a[href$='.pdf']
patterns.notes: Visible but not downloaded in bounded smoke.

robots.notes: Scoped issue and PDF routes are not disallowed.

barriers.notes: Sidebar includes mislabeled/mismatched CLR slugs and neighboring-journal PDF Archive navigation; validate h1 and path on every issue.

access.politeness_notes: Robots first; one homepage, newest issue, and article PDF smoke only.

verdict.summary: NYU's visible 2010-2026 CLR archive has deterministic issue pages and direct article PDFs; the newest smoke passes, but older layouts and the pre-2010 backfile remain unresolved.
blockers: Sidebar labels and slugs contain defects
Abstracts and pages absent from HTML
verdict.next_action: Implement the scoped Drupal issue parser, sample one middle and oldest visible issue, then locate the pre-2010 backfile before acquisition.
verdict.implementation_notes: Title is the paragraph text node and author is the PDF anchor text; reject whole-issue rows from article corpus and validate issue h1 against sidebar labels.
